title: Double-Side Cooled SiC MOSFET Power Modules With Sintered-Silver Interposers for a 100-kW/L Traction Inverter
authors_or_org: Chao Ding, Shengchang Lu, Zichen Zhang, Kun Zhang, Tam Nguyen, Khai D. T. Ngo, Rolando Burgos, Guo-Quan Lu (Center for Power Electronics Systems, Virginia Tech)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 38, no. 8, pp. 9685-9694, Aug 2023): double-side-cooled SiC MOSFET power module with sintered-silver interposers reduces power-loop parasitic inductance by ~23% and increases power-handling capability by ~44% vs. a prior double-side-cooled design, targeting a 100 kW/L EV traction inverter.
locator: IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 38, no. 8, pp. 9685-9694 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: New L02 sub-niche vs. existing ledger (L02-001..054 cover SST, device reliability, 48V-to-<1V bus converters, planar transformers): EV traction-inverter power-module packaging/double-side cooling. Crossref metadata fetched and cross-checked this session (title/DOI/volume/issue/pages match); IEEE Xplore full text not independently fetched (Xplore has blocked automated fetches in prior L02 sessions per L02_power_electronics.md).

title: A High-Power Density Segmented Traction Drive Inverter
authors_or_org: Gui-Jia Su, Jon Wilkins, Lingxiao Xue, Burak Ozpineci, Raj Sahu, Emre Gurpinar (Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Buildings and Transportation Science Division)
claim_supported: ORNL-authored 2023 IEEE ECCE proceedings paper (pp. 1825-1830, confirmed via ORNL's own publication page and via Crossref): a segmented traction-drive inverter using low-profile double-sided-cooled SiC MOSFET modules and genetic-algorithm-optimized mini-channel heat sinks achieves total inverter volume <1 L, exceeding a 100 kW/L power-density goal for EV traction drives.
locator: 2023 IEEE ECCE proceedings, pp. 1825-1830 (ORNL publication page fetched; Crossref bibliographic record cross-checked)
notes: National-lab-authored (ORNL) primary technical paper, published as peer-reviewed IEEE conference proceedings -- distinct sub-niche (segmented traction-inverter topology / capacitor reduction) from existing L02 device-reliability and SST records. Both ORNL publication page and Crossref record fetched and cross-checked this session; IEEE Xplore full text not independently fetched.

title: Coupled Inductors With an Adaptive Coupling Coefficient for Multiphase Voltage Regulators
authors_or_org: F. Zhu, Q. Li (Center for Power Electronics Systems, Virginia Tech)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 38, no. 1, pp. 739-749, 2023): proposes a coupled-inductor structure for multiphase voltage regulator modules using a variable inductor in the extra winding loop so the coupling coefficient self-adjusts, improving transient response without increasing phase-inductor current ripple -- targets the steady-state/transient tradeoff limiting VRM power density.
locator: IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 38, no. 1, pp. 739-749 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: New high-density-DC-DC/magnetics sub-niche (multiphase VRM coupled-inductor design) distinct from existing L02 planar-transformer (L02-019/020) and 48V-to-<1V bus-converter (L02-022/023/024) records. Author affiliation (Virginia Tech CPES) inferred from a CPES library listing found via search, not from a Crossref affiliation field (Crossref returned no affiliation data for this record) -- flagged for verifier confirmation.

title: An Improved Hybrid-Coupled Inductor Structure With Flux Reduction and Integrated Controllable Coupling Function
authors_or_org: F. Zhu, X. Lou, Q. Li (Center for Power Electronics Systems, Virginia Tech)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 39, no. 1, pp. 1103-1114, 2024): splits magnetic flux across different core-plate areas, allowing a thinner plate and smaller inductor size while flexibly controlling the coupling coefficient via per-leg flux -- a further multiphase-VRM magnetics power-density improvement, companion to L02-103.
locator: IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 39, no. 1, pp. 1103-1114 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: Same VRM-magnetics sub-niche and author group as L02-103; kept as a separate canonical work (different DOI/volume/content, an iterative follow-on design) rather than merged, per dedupe-by-canonical-key rule. Affiliation inferred the same way as L02-103, not confirmed via Crossref.

title: Design of a GaN Totem-Pole PFC Converter Using DC-Link Voltage Control Strategy for Data Center Applications
authors_or_org: Jia-You Lee (National Cheng Kung University), Jheng-Hung Chen, Kuo-Yuan Lo (National Kaohsiung University of Science and Technology)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (IEEE Access, vol. 10, pp. 50278-50287, 2022): a GaN HEMT totem-pole PFC converter with a DC-link voltage control strategy for data-center applications achieves 98.5% peak efficiency and <3% THD in a 2.9 kW prototype, using a hold-up-time-extension method to raise DC-bus voltage under heavy load and shrink DC-link capacitance.
locator: IEEE Access, vol. 10, pp. 50278-50287 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: New datacenter sub-niche: the AC-DC PFC/rectifier front-end stage -- existing L02 datacenter records (L02-037..054) cover 800VDC rack architecture and 48V-to-<1V point-of-load conversion but not the upstream PFC stage. Author affiliations (NCKU/NKUST, Taiwan) found via a secondary ResearchGate/profile search, not from Crossref affiliation metadata.

title: Design Considerations of a GaN-based Three-Level Traction Inverter for Electric Vehicles
authors_or_org: Subhransu Satpathy, Partha Pratim Das, Subhashish Bhattacharya (North Carolina State University), Victor Veliadis (PowerAmerica)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (2022 IEEE WiPDA proceedings, pp. 192-197): designs and evaluates a GaN-based three-level active-neutral-point-clamped (3L-ANPC) traction inverter operating on an 800V DC bus for EV powertrains, addressing device/topology tradeoffs specific to WBG EV drivetrain electronics.
locator: 2022 IEEE WiPDA proceedings, pp. 192-197 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: New EV-drivetrain sub-niche (three-level GaN traction-inverter topology for 800V EV buses) distinct from existing L02 SST/fast-charging and device-reliability records. Victor Veliadis is publicly identified as PowerAmerica's CTO; affiliation noted from public bio, not independently verified in Crossref metadata.

title: Single-Stage Multiport Solid-State Transformer Based Medium-Voltage DC Interconnection System and Coordinated Control Strategies
authors_or_org: Di Zhu, Chuang Liu, Jingyue Wang, Heyang Sun, Yu Jiang (Northeast Electric Power University, Jilin, China); Zhongchen Pei (Dalian University of Technology, China); Chao Liu (Technical University of Denmark)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 41, no. 2, pp. 2847-2859): proposes a single-stage multiport solid-state transformer for medium-voltage DC (MVDC) interconnection with coordinated multi-port control strategies -- a grid-scale (not EV-charging) SST application distinct from existing L02 SST records.
locator: IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 41, no. 2, pp. 2847-2859 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: New sub-niche: grid-scale MVDC-interconnection SST, vs. existing L02 SST records which target EV extreme-fast-charging or MV-AC-to-400VDC. Crossref assigns this to vol. 41 no. 2, dated 2026 (the DOI itself carries a 2025 registration year); flagging this online-first/issue-date ambiguity rather than asserting a single year with false confidence.

title: Development of SiC Power Module Structure by Micron-Sized Ag-Paste Sinter Joining on Both Die and Heatsink to Low-Thermal-Resistance and Superior Power Cycling Reliability
authors_or_org: Chuantong Chen, Aiji Suetake, Fupeng Huo, Dongjin Kim, Zheng Zhang, Ming-Chun Hsieh, Wanli Li, Naoki Wakasugi, Kazutaka Takeshita, Yoshiji Yamaguchi, Yashima Momose, Katsuaki Suganuma
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 39, no. 9, pp. 10638-10650, Sept. 2024): develops a SiC power-module structure using micron-sized Ag-paste sinter joining on both die and heatsink, achieving low thermal resistance and improved power-cycling reliability -- a die-attach/packaging failure-mode sub-niche distinct from the existing L02 capacitor-reliability and gate-driver records.
locator: IEEE Trans. Power Electronics, vol. 39, no. 9, pp. 10638-10650 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: Author-group affiliation (Osaka University, Japan) inferred from Katsuaki Suganuma's well-documented public research-group identity (sinter-joining packaging research); not independently confirmed via a Crossref affiliation field this session, so geography should be re-checked by the verifier.

title: A Bidirectional GaN-Based CLLC Converter for Plug-In Electric Vehicles On-Board Chargers
authors_or_org: Ahmed M. Ammar, Kawsar Ali, Daniel J. Rogers (University of Oxford)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (IECON 2020 proceedings, pp. 1129-1135): presents a bidirectional GaN-based CLLC DC-DC converter for plug-in EV on-board chargers using 650V GaN devices on both sides, achieving up to 95.7% efficiency with soft switching across the operating range and resonant inductance integrated into the transformer.
locator: IECON 2020 proceedings, pp. 1129-1135 (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session)
notes: New EV sub-niche: bidirectional (V2G-capable) on-board-charger DC-DC stage, distinct from existing L02 traction-inverter and fast-charging-SST records. Daniel J. Rogers is a publicly documented University of Oxford power-electronics researcher; affiliation not independently confirmed via Crossref (no affiliation field returned).

title: 400V-to-48V GaN Modular LLC Resonant Converter with Planar Transformers
authors_or_org: Qingyun Huang, Qingxuan Ma, Alex Q. Huang (University of Texas at Austin); Michael de Rooij (Efficient Power Conversion Corp.)
claim_supported: Crossref-confirmed record (2021 IEEE ECCE proceedings): a modular 400V-to-48V GaN LLC resonant converter using planar transformers, targeting high-power-density datacenter bus conversion -- the 400V-bus-to-48V stage upstream of the 48V-to-<1V records already in the L02 ledger.
locator: 2021 IEEE ECCE proceedings (bibliographic record confirmed via Crossref API this session; exact page range not returned by Crossref)
notes: New sub-niche bridging high-frequency planar magnetics (existing L02-019/020) and datacenter bus conversion (existing L02-022/023/024) at the 400V-to-48V stage. Industry co-authorship (Efficient Power Conversion Corp., a GaN device vendor) noted; treated as academic because it is peer-reviewed IEEE proceedings, not vendor marketing collateral.

title: Energy Department Advances U.S. Electric Grid Resilience and Reliability with New Transformer and Silicon Carbide Packaging Projects
authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy, Office of Electricity
claim_supported: Fetched DOE Office of Electricity article (dated Dec 3, 2024): announces ~$20M across nine Flexible Innovative Transformer Technologies (FITT) projects (Clemson $2.47M SHAPE, Eaton $2.94M Voltage Regulating Hybrid, EPRI $2.88M 'Solid State PowerHub', two GE Vernova awards $1.99M/$2.99M, Univ. of Pittsburgh $2.5M, three more at $1.45-1.84M) plus 8 winners of Phase 1 of a $2.25M Silicon Carbide (SiC) Packaging Prize ($50,000 each) advancing toward physical prototypes tested at national labs.
locator: Article body: FITT project list paragraph and SiC Packaging Prize Phase 1 winners paragraph
notes: New government sub-niche vs. existing L02 ledger (ARPA-E CIRCUITS/DC-GRIDS programs, DOE AMMTO WBG strategic framework draft, Sandia fact sheet): this is DOE's Office of Electricity grid-modernization/transformer program, a distinct DOE office and program line from ARPA-E, funding both distribution-transformer R&D (including an explicit 'Solid State PowerHub' SST project) and SiC power-module packaging R&D directly relevant to grid-scale power conversion. Page fetched and quoted this session.

title: IEC 62477-1:2022 -- Safety requirements for power electronic converter systems and equipment -- Part 1: General
claim_supported: Fetched IEC webstore record confirms IEC 62477-1:2022, Edition 2.0, published 2022-05-20: sets minimum safety requirements (fire, electric shock, thermal, energy, mechanical hazards) for power electronic converter systems (PECS) rated up to 1000V AC / 1500V DC; serves as the horizontal safety standard referenced by solar, wind, fuel-cell, and general power-converter product standards, explicitly excluding telecom apparatus (except power supplies), IEC 61508 functional safety, and railway/EV-traction systems.
locator: IEC webstore publication page 28936, scope/edition/date fields
notes: New standards sub-niche vs. existing L02 ledger, which only carries IEC 60076-24 (transformer-specific test standard, cited as an SST compliance bottleneck). IEC 62477-1 is the general horizontal safety standard for power-electronic converter systems/equipment broadly -- directly relevant to every WBG converter, SST, and datacenter power-conversion product in this lane's scope. Geography listed as global-adoption markets rather than a single jurisdiction since IEC standards are adopted multi-nationally (verifier should refine if a single-country lens is required).

title: Vicor Corporation Form 10-K (Fiscal Year Ended December 31, 2025)
claim_supported: Vicor's FY2025 10-K (SEC-accepted March 2, 2026, 2:38pm) describes Advanced Products (Factorized Power Architecture, Power-on-Package for AI processors/GPUs, vertical power delivery) targeting 'high-performance computing, AI processors, and automotive electrification,' vs. Brick Products serving 'aerospace and defense electronics, industrial equipment, instrumentation and test equipment, and transportation'; Advanced Products (incl. licensing) were ~61.0% of FY2025 consolidated net revenue and Brick Products ~39.0%; licensing revenue was $57,384,000 including a $45M patent-litigation settlement.
locator: Item 1 Business (product-line descriptions and revenue-mix percentages), as reproduced/quoted in a StockTitan SEC-filing summary of the same 10-K
notes: New company-filing sub-niche vs. existing L02 ledger (Navitas 8-K, Wolfspeed earnings-call transcript): Vicor is a high-density modular-power/DC-DC specialist distinct from the WBG-device makers already covered, directly matching the lane's 'high-density DC-DC' core scope. HONESTY NOTE: direct WebFetch of the SEC.gov URL returned HTTP 403 (and a retry against Vicor's own investor-relations static-file mirror timed out/reset) this session, so the quoted figures were verified via a StockTitan article that reproduces the filing's text and confirms the SEC acceptance date/accession path -- fetched:true refers to that verification fetch, not a direct render of the SEC.gov page itself. url/canonical_key point to the actual SEC EDGAR filing (accession 0001193125-26-085102) as the authoritative primary source.

title: GE Vernova and Seatrium Secure a Major Contract from TenneT to Connect North Sea Wind Power to Germany's Grid (BalWin5 HVDC)
authors_or_org: GE Vernova (press release); buyer: TenneT TSO GmbH
claim_supported: Fetched press release (dated Dec 11, 2025): German TSO TenneT awarded the GE Vernova-Seatrium consortium the BalWin5 HVDC project under their existing framework agreement -- full HVDC technology plus control/protection systems, an onshore converter station at Bremen-Werderland, an offshore converter station/platform in the North Sea, and a 325km combined cable system, rated 2.2GW (plus 200MW technical headroom), commissioning targeted for 2032; the fourth project awarded to this consortium under the framework.
locator: Press release body: project scope, rating, and framework-agreement paragraphs
notes: Direct grid-scale power-conversion buyer-procurement record (a TSO's HVDC converter-station contract award) -- the lane's 'grid-scale... power conversion' sub-scope was otherwise represented only by government R&D-program records (ARPA-E DC-GRIDS) and academic MVDC-SST papers, not by an actual utility capital-procurement action. Source is a vendor (GE Vernova) press release reporting a buyer's (TenneT) award, not the buyer's own tender notice, since TenneT's own tender pages returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch this session -- flagged as vendor-side reporting of a real contract, not vendor marketing of a hypothetical product.
